description: Schema migration adds `contracts.owner_client_id` and keeps template contracts unaffected
Type wiring exposes `owner_client_id` on contract interfaces used by billing actions and UI loaders
Preflight query identifies shared non-template contracts with more than one distinct client
Selection rule preserves the only invoiced assignment when a shared contract has invoice history on exactly one assignment
Selection rule preserves the earliest-starting assignment when a shared contract has no invoice history
Migration backfills `owner_client_id` on preserved non-template contracts to the preserved assignment client
Migration clones contract header rows with fresh `contract_id` values for every non-preserved assignment
Migration clones all source `contract_lines` to the new contract with fresh line IDs and preserved descriptive/rate fields
Migration clones required contract-line child/configuration rows so cloned contracts remain billable
Migration repoints non-preserved `client_contracts.contract_id` rows to the cloned contract IDs
DB-backed sanity: preserved invoiced assignment still retains the original `contract_id` after migration
Migration aborts with a clear error when a clone-target assignment has unsupported historical references that would require retargeting
Non-template contract creation via supported app flow requires an owner client
Attempting to create a non-template contract without owner client fails outside migration/internal exemption paths
Assigning a client-owned contract to a different client than `owner_client_id` is rejected
Updating an existing assignment to point at a contract owned by a different client is rejected
Renewal draft creation copies the source client as `owner_client_id` on the draft contract
Legacy API/service path cannot create a new cross-client shared non-template contract after guardrails are added
Contract model/list actions no longer treat contracts without client ownership as normal live client contracts
Contract lifecycle helpers no longer derive live multi-client status by aggregating unrelated client assignments
Billing engine still resolves billable lines correctly for preserved and cloned assignments after migration
DB-backed integration: invoice generation succeeds for a migrated cloned assignment with the same cloned contract lines
Client contract lifecycle helper remains the source of truth for active/terminated/expired display state
ClientContractsTab loads assignment-first rows and does not rely on `getContractsWithClients()` shared-contract semantics
ClientContractsTab status badge reflects assignment lifecycle when contract-header status differs
ClientContractsTab live actions mutate assignment lifecycle instead of contract-header active/terminated state
Client contract detail route can open the correct live contract using `clientContractId` as the primary context
Contract detail view shows owner/assignment-derived semantics and no longer implies a non-template contract can span unrelated clients
Templates UI still lists and edits reusable templates independently of client-owned contract instances
Contract revenue action uses `client_contracts` as the fact source for live assigned contracts
Contract expiration action/report derives active/expiring rows from assignment truth rather than `contracts.is_active`
Report results for the two known shared contracts no longer appear as one shared contract spanning two clients after migration
API responses/documentation for `/contracts` reflect client-owned instantiated contract headers
Documentation updates describe templates as the only reusable concept and non-template contracts as client-owned instances
DB-backed post-migration sanity: no non-template contract is linked to more than one distinct client
DB-backed post-migration sanity: the preserved `Managed IT Services` assignment for The Green Thumb still has its invoice history intact
DB-backed post-migration sanity: `Managed IT Services` for BTM Machinery points at a new cloned contract ID
DB-backed post-migration sanity: `Worry-Free Essentials` for The Benjamin Wolf Group points at a new cloned contract ID
DB-backed post-migration sanity: preserved `Worry-Free Essentials` contract remains on WorryNot Works IT Services as the earliest-start assignment
DB-backed post-migration sanity: cloned contracts have the same contract-line counts as their source contracts
No current production clone-target assignments require invoice/doc/time/usage retargeting, matching the migration preflight assumptions
Resuming a draft contract still works when the draft contract is client-owned and its live status is not inferred from shared contract semantics
Renewals queue still creates and links draft contracts correctly after owner-client enforcement
API/service contract-line mutation paths continue to work for client-owned non-template contracts after cloning and owner enforcement
Client portal contract-linked document queries still return the correct documents for the owning client after a shared contract is split
Client portal contract-linked document queries do not leak a migrated cloned contract's documents to the other client that used to share the original contract
Client contract line overlap checks do not surface another client's contract-derived lines after a shared contract is split
Client contract line listing returns only the requesting client's contract-derived lines for preserved and cloned contracts after migration
